questions.question: What is the topic for Week 4?
options: D. FUNCTIONS OF THE HOLY SPIRIT
questions.explanation: The stated topic for Lesson Four is HODEGEO PNEUMA.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 3?
options: D. THE WAY, THE TRUTH, AND THE LIFE
questions.explanation: The Deity of Jesus (Part 2) was the topic for Week 3.

questions.question: According to the introduction, what does the Greek word 'Hodegeo' mean?
questions.explanation: The introduction states: 'Hodegeo is a Greek word meaning guidance'.

questions.question: What does the Greek word 'Pneuma' translate to in English according to the lesson?
questions.explanation: The text says 'while Pneuma is the Holy Spirit.'

questions.question: However, when He, the Spirit of truth, has come, He will ________ you into all truth...
questions.explanation: The memory verse (John 16:13) says 'He will guide you into all truth'.

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is John 16:13.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 4 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: However, when He, the Spirit of truth, has come, He will guide you into all truth; for He will not speak on His own authority, but whatever He hears He will speak; and He will tell you things to come
questions.explanation: Full text recall of John 16:13.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (John 16:13), on whose authority will the Spirit of truth speak?
options: C. He will not speak on His own authority, but whatever He hears
questions.explanation: The verse says 'for He will not speak on His own authority, but whatever He hears He will speak'.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (John 16:13), the Spirit of truth will tell you things of the past, but not things to come.
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states 'and He will tell you things to come'.

questions.question: Jesus told His disciples that His Father would send the ________ – the Holy Spirit.
questions.explanation: The intro says 'His Father would send the Comforter – the Holy Spirit'.

questions.question: According to Ephesians 1:13-14 cited in the introduction, when does the Holy Spirit become a part of a person's life?
options: B. Immediately when a person believes in Christ
D. After a period of fasting
questions.explanation: The intro states: 'Now, when a person believes in Christ, the Holy Spirit immediately becomes a part of his life (Ephesians 1:13-14).'

questions.question: Which biblical event fulfilled Jesus' promise that the Holy Spirit would come with power?
questions.explanation: The text states 'fulfilled when the Holy Spirit came with power on believers at Pentecost (Acts 2).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline in the Week 4 lesson?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'FUNCTIONS OF THE HOLY SPIRIT'.

questions.question: According to 1 Corinthians 12:7-11, what is one of the functions of the Holy Spirit?
options: A. He distributes spiritual gifts according to His will
B. He reproves the world of sin
C. He seals us until the day of return
questions.explanation: The lesson points to 1 Cor 12:7-11 for the fact that 'He distributes spiritual gifts according to His will'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, the Holy Spirit distributes spiritual gifts according to our own personal desires and will.
questions.explanation: It says 'He distributes spiritual gifts according to His will'.

questions.question: Based on John 16:8 cited in Outline I, what function does the Holy Spirit perform?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'reproves (John 16:8)'.

questions.question: Where is the function of the Holy Spirit to 'teach and remind' found in the references given in Outline I?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'teaches and reminds (John 14:26)'.

questions.question: The Holy Spirit remains in us as a ________ of promise upon our hearts until the day of Jesus’ return (Ephesians 4:30).
questions.explanation: The text explicitly says 'seal of promise upon our hearts' citing Eph 4:30.

questions.question: In His role as Guide and Counsellor, what does the Holy Spirit reveal to us according to Outline I?
options: A. The future of the world economy
D. The exact date of Christ's return
questions.explanation: The outline states: 'leading us in the way we should go and revealing God’s truth'.

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline in the Week 4 lesson?
questions.answer: OUR THOUGHTS VERSUS THE HOLY SPIRIT’S LEADING
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'OUR THOUGHTS VERSUS THE HOLY SPIRIT’S LEADING'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, fear is a product of uncontrolled thoughts.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Fear is a product of uncontrolled thoughts.'

questions.question: Which Bible reference supports the idea that our thoughts may be sensual?
questions.explanation: The lesson says 'Our thoughts may be sensual (Romans 8:5).'

questions.question: What does the lesson say we should do even when our thought seems positive?
options: B. Check with the Holy Spirit
C. Ask our friends for advice
questions.explanation: The text states: 'It is important to check with the Holy Spirit even when the thought seems positive.'

questions.question: The Holy Spirit sometimes guides us through our ________ (Romans 9:1).
questions.explanation: The lesson notes: 'The Holy Spirit sometimes guides us through our conscience (Romans 9:1).'

questions.question: What is described as one of the most important ways to recognise the Holy Spirit's guidance (supported by 2 Timothy 3:16)?
questions.explanation: The text says 'The word of God is one of the most important ways to recognise the Holy Spirit’s guidance'.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that knowledge of God's word helps us discern whether our desires come from the Holy Spirit?
questions.explanation: The text links this directly to '(Psalm 119:11)'.

questions.question: What does the leading of the Holy Spirit bring, according to Romans 15:13?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Tho leading of the Holy Spirit also brings peace (Romans 15:13).' Note: typo in original text 'pea' meant 'peace'.

questions.question: When we know the will of God but do not follow it, we are resisting the Spirit’s work in our lives.
questions.explanation: The lesson states exactly this, citing Acts 7:51 and 1 Thess 5:19.

questions.question: According to Ephesians 4:30 as used in Outline II, what is the result of following our own ways instead of God's will?
options: B. It grieves the Holy Spirit
D. It removes the Spirit from us
questions.explanation: The text says 'When we follow our ways, it grieves Him (Ephesians 4:30).'

questions.question: ________ sin will cause us to miss what the Holy Spirit wants to say to us through the word.
questions.explanation: The lesson explicitly warns: 'Habitual sin will cause us to miss what the Holy Spirit wants to say...'

questions.question: What does the conclusion of the lesson urge believers to do?
options: A. Be constantly in tune with the Holy Spirit
B. Fast and pray for 40 days
C. Speak in tongues every morning
questions.explanation: The conclusion is a simple command: 'Be constantly in tune with the Holy Spirit.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? '“I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.'
questions.verse_text: “I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
questions.explanation: This is verse 12 from the main Bible Passage, John 16:12-14.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (John 16:14), how will the Spirit of truth glorify Jesus?
options: A. By performing miracles in His name
B. By taking what is His and declaring it to the disciples
C. By making His enemies His footstool
questions.explanation: The verse says 'He will glorify Me, for He will take of what is Mine and declare it to you.'

questions.question: The lesson teaches that our thoughts can be unstable and misleading, and that they often wander from one end to another.
questions.explanation: Outline II states: 'Thoughts can be unstable and sometimes misleading. It wanders from one end to another.'

questions.question: Which scriptures are given to support the necessity of being in continual prayer (in the Spirit) to keep our hearts open to the Spirit's leading?
questions.explanation: The lesson pairs '(1 Thessalonians 5:17; Romans 8:26-27)' with this point.
